---
title: "Elliott's Yelloweyed Grass (Xyris elliottii)"
slug: "xyris-elliottii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/476399293/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Elliott's Yelloweyed Grass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Xyridaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Xyridaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/476399293/medium.jpg" alt="Elliott's Yelloweyed Grass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/garccyy">
garccyy</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/265242159">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Xyridaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=132684)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A slender wetland plant characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and bright yellow flower heads borne on tall stalks. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as bogs, wet flatwoods, and savannas. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is most noticeable when in flower, attracting small pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/132684-Xyris-elliottii)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/xyris-elliottii/)
